mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present values of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during rate cuts show no statistical significance above baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth stays below 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential investment demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail and housing data fails to predict negative quarterly returns when either series rebounds within two months, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production confirms a stabilization in manufacturing output, which drives positive earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crises.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with month-over-month payroll declines signal a liquidity trap and deteriorating economic activity, causing investors to price in prolonged stagnation and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of sub-1% rates and falling payrolls yields positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when payroll declines are < 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 220.0 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: CPI readings above 220.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the threshold effect disappears when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising consumer income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns when growth exceeds 200k, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real gross domestic product confirms a contraction in aggregate economic activity, prompting downward earnings revisions and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth coincides with positive 20-day equity returns in > 65% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when the decline is < 0.5%.
